surface_or_claim: Kolm can capture OpenAI and Anthropic-compatible traffic today.
official_or_code_evidence: `kolm capture` writes config; `/v1/capture/openai` and `/v1/capture/anthropic` proxy calls and record observations.
kolm_code_truth: This is a real source-owned trace path, but only for direct provider proxying.
risk_or_opportunity: Use as baseline for importer schema and trace normalization.
recommended_action: Keep current capture path as the local source baseline.

surface_or_claim: Kolm can export captured pairs as labels.
official_or_code_evidence: `kolm labels` calls `/v1/labels/synthesize-corpus` and can write JSONL or JSON from observations.
kolm_code_truth: This provides the first internal trace-to-dataset primitive.
risk_or_opportunity: Treat this as the local target format for external importer fixtures.
recommended_action: Use labels export as the local target fixture.

surface_or_claim: Kolm has external competitor importers.
official_or_code_evidence: Source search found no `kolm import` command or competitor-specific connector for Langfuse, LangSmith, Braintrust, Helicone, Phoenix, Weave, OpenPipe, LiteLLM, Vercel, Cloudflare, or Portkey.
kolm_code_truth: The strongest competitor wedge is not implemented.
risk_or_opportunity: Imported competitor traces are the highest-leverage acquisition path.
recommended_action: Add `kolm import <source>` with fixtures before building more dashboard UI.

surface_or_claim: Kolm has a source-neutral trace/eval schema.
official_or_code_evidence: Local code stores observations with provider-specific prompt/response fields and no canonical external trace row schema.
kolm_code_truth: Importer work will fragment without a shared schema.
risk_or_opportunity: Schema-first import prevents one-off connector drift.
recommended_action: Create `kolm-trace-1` with source IDs, input, output, expected, scores, model, cost, latency, consent, and redaction fields.

surface_or_claim: Langfuse can feed traces and observations into Kolm.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs expose public APIs, SDK trace list/get, observations, scores, datasets, metrics, manual UI export, and blob storage export.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Langfuse importer.
risk_or_opportunity: Langfuse is the best first importer because it is open-source and source-compatible with self-hosted buyers.
recommended_action: Build Langfuse query/export fixture and normalization tests first.

surface_or_claim: Langfuse scores and datasets can become eval cases.
official_or_code_evidence: Langfuse SDK docs expose `api.datasets`, `api.dataset_items`, scores, trace queries, and observation queries.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm K-score does not consume Langfuse score/rubric metadata.
risk_or_opportunity: External eval evidence would be lost or hand-mapped.
recommended_action: Map Langfuse scores and dataset items into Kolm eval cases with provenance.

surface_or_claim: LangSmith can export trace runs at scale.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe bulk trace export to S3-compatible buckets in Parquet format with fields for run IDs, trace hierarchy, inputs, outputs, tags, feedback, costs, and tokens.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no LangSmith bulk import path.
risk_or_opportunity: Enterprise LangChain users already have source trace data Kolm could compile from.
recommended_action: Add LangSmith Parquet/SDK importer after Langfuse.

surface_or_claim: LangSmith traces can be queried without bulk export.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs recommend `list_runs` SDK or `/runs/query` API for trace queries and bulk export for high volume.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no API/SDK pull mode.
risk_or_opportunity: Small proof fixtures can use query APIs before bulk export plumbing.
recommended_action: Start with SDK/query fixture, then add Parquet bulk mode.

surface_or_claim: Braintrust can feed datasets, experiments, logs, and scorers.
official_or_code_evidence: Official API docs cover projects, experiments, datasets, production logs, prompts, tools, scorers, and results.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Braintrust importer or scorer mapping.
risk_or_opportunity: Braintrust is close to K-score workflow; Kolm must consume rather than duplicate it.
recommended_action: Map Braintrust datasets and scores into artifact eval gates.

surface_or_claim: Braintrust BTQL can retrieve high-scoring traces.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs show BTQL queries over project logs with input, output, and scores fields.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no BTQL adapter.
risk_or_opportunity: BTQL could select high-confidence compile candidates quickly.
recommended_action: Build a BTQL example query and convert rows to `kolm-trace-1`.

surface_or_claim: Helicone datasets can become Kolm examples.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe selecting requests into datasets and exporting for fine-tuning or analysis in JSONL/CSV formats.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Helicone dataset importer.
risk_or_opportunity: Helicone owns a practical request-log-to-dataset path Kolm can consume.
recommended_action: Build Helicone dataset import as second importer.

surface_or_claim: Helicone has bulk request export tooling.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe request query APIs and an `npx` export CLI with JSONL/CSV and body inclusion options.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Helicone export reader.
risk_or_opportunity: A Helicone JSONL path can become the fastest real-traffic proof.
recommended_action: Normalize Helicone export rows to `kolm-trace-1`.

surface_or_claim: Phoenix can import and export traces.
official_or_code_evidence: Official Phoenix docs describe trace import/export, CLI trace export, REST access, OTLP collection, datasets, experiments, and evals.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Phoenix importer.
risk_or_opportunity: Phoenix is open-source adjacent and useful for self-hosted teams.
recommended_action: Add Phoenix CLI/REST import after Langfuse and Helicone.

surface_or_claim: W&B Weave can export eval results.
official_or_code_evidence: Official Weave docs describe Evaluation REST API endpoints for evaluation runs, predictions, resolved dataset inputs, scores, scorer stats, and row digests.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Weave eval importer.
risk_or_opportunity: Weave row digests map well to artifact provenance and deduplication.
recommended_action: Add Weave eval import as an eval-gate source.

surface_or_claim: LiteLLM can send trace data to Kolm via callbacks.
official_or_code_evidence: Official LiteLLM docs expose success/failure/input callbacks and custom logger hooks with model, messages, cost, cache hit, response, and timing fields.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no LiteLLM callback package.
risk_or_opportunity: LiteLLM sits in production traffic and can feed compile candidates.
recommended_action: Ship a LiteLLM callback that writes `kolm-trace-1` rows or posts to Kolm capture.

surface_or_claim: LiteLLM proxy hooks can support response metadata or routing experiments.
official_or_code_evidence: LiteLLM docs list proxy-only pre/post hooks that can modify requests or responses.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no proxy hook integration.
risk_or_opportunity: A route-first proof could attach artifact receipts to responses.
recommended_action: Prototype post-success hook for avoided-call and receipt metadata.

surface_or_claim: LiteLLM supports custom API server providers.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs include a custom API server provider surface and broad provider list.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no OpenAI-compatible artifact runtime endpoint packaged for LiteLLM.
risk_or_opportunity: A custom provider is possible only after Kolm exposes a stable runtime API shape.
recommended_action: Define a Kolm OpenAI-compatible provider target after sandbox/trust work.

surface_or_claim: Vercel AI SDK can route through middleware.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs for `wrapLanguageModel` show middleware wrapping a model and returning a new language model instance.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no AI SDK middleware package.
risk_or_opportunity: Next.js/AI SDK apps can try artifacts before model fallback with minimal app changes.
recommended_action: Build `kolm-ai-sdk-middleware` after artifact runtime API stabilizes.

surface_or_claim: Cloudflare AI Gateway can target custom providers.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe creating custom providers with base URL, slug, OpenAI-compatible `/compat` routing, provider-specific endpoints, and HTTPS base URL requirements.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Cloudflare custom-provider guide or compatible hosted runtime target.
risk_or_opportunity: Cloudflare can become a distribution channel if Kolm exposes a safe endpoint.
recommended_action: Add Cloudflare custom-provider guide after public-run trust tiers are fixed.

surface_or_claim: Portkey can route to private/self-hosted providers.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe virtual/provider keys and privately hosted provider support with custom host fields.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no Portkey provider guide or route target.
risk_or_opportunity: Portkey users could route deterministic traffic to Kolm if endpoint semantics are clear.
recommended_action: Document Kolm as a private provider only after runtime auth and receipts are stable.

surface_or_claim: OpenPipe can export request datasets as JSONL.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe raw JSONL export with messages, tools, tool choice, and split fields.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no OpenPipe import path.
risk_or_opportunity: OpenPipe data is highly compatible with Kolm examples and eval splits.
recommended_action: Build OpenPipe JSONL import and compare output to hosted fine-tune path.

surface_or_claim: OpenPipe directly owns trace-to-cheaper-model replacement.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs say OpenPipe collects logs, creates fine-tuned models, evaluates them, and hosts OpenAI-format replacements.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm cannot yet match model replacement because recipe/model-adapter artifacts are incomplete.
risk_or_opportunity: OpenPipe is the direct wedge competitor for cost and latency from production traces.
recommended_action: Differentiate on signed portable artifacts, receipts, and local/private governance.

surface_or_claim: Predibase owns the adapter training/deployment path.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs describe uploading datasets, creating adapters, evaluating, and serving LoRA adapters from deployments.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm local tune/promotion path is not equivalent to Predibase's hosted adapter workflow.
risk_or_opportunity: Predibase can be a training backend or competitor depending on Kolm artifact tier truth.
recommended_action: Treat Predibase as backend until Kolm model-adapter artifacts are inspectable.

surface_or_claim: Predibase makes generic LoRA claims risky for Kolm.
official_or_code_evidence: Predibase product/docs expose mature fine-tuning, adapter, deployment, and structured output paths.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm public LoRA claims remain ahead of artifact evidence.
risk_or_opportunity: Buyers can compare unshipped Kolm adapter claims to shipped Predibase workflows.
recommended_action: Keep LoRA public copy roadmap unless artifacts prove adapter contents and runtime.

surface_or_claim: Kolm writes receipts back to source systems.
official_or_code_evidence: No local code found for writing artifact IDs, K-scores, receipts, or avoided-call metrics back into Langfuse, LangSmith, Braintrust, Helicone, Phoenix, Weave, LiteLLM, or Vercel.
kolm_code_truth: Importers without write-back make Kolm invisible in existing workflows.
risk_or_opportunity: Imported artifacts need to reappear in the buyer's existing trace system.
recommended_action: Add source write-back metadata after first import proof.

surface_or_claim: Kolm has source-specific import redaction/consent controls.
official_or_code_evidence: Local capture stores prompt/response text and importer schema does not yet exist.
kolm_code_truth: Imported traces can include regulated or customer-sensitive data.
risk_or_opportunity: Trace import can pull regulated data into Kolm by default.
recommended_action: Require redaction mode, consent flags, retention, and hash-only options in `kolm-trace-1`.

surface_or_claim: All competitor importers have equal priority.
official_or_code_evidence: Official docs show different friction: Langfuse and Helicone have easy APIs/exports; LangSmith bulk export is plan-gated; Braintrust/Weave are eval-rich; Cloudflare/Portkey require stable hosted targets.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has no prioritization artifact yet.
risk_or_opportunity: Building all connectors at once will dilute proof.
recommended_action: Prioritize Langfuse, Helicone, LiteLLM callback, LangSmith, Braintrust, then Vercel middleware.

surface_or_claim: Importer fixtures exist for top competitor sources.
official_or_code_evidence: No fixture files or tests were found for external trace exports.
kolm_code_truth: Importer claims would be untestable.
risk_or_opportunity: Fixtures are needed before public importer claims are credible.
recommended_action: Add small golden fixtures for Langfuse, Helicone, LangSmith, Braintrust, and OpenPipe.

surface_or_claim: Kolm can prove avoided calls from imported traces.
official_or_code_evidence: Local benchmark receipts do not join back to imported trace sources or original costs.
kolm_code_truth: Savings claims lack trace-to-artifact accounting.
risk_or_opportunity: Imported traces need original cost context to prove replacement value.
recommended_action: Add avoided-call report fields: source call count, model, cost, artifact latency, fallback count, and receipt IDs.

surface_or_claim: Kolm should compete by rebuilding trace/eval/gateway products.
official_or_code_evidence: Competitor docs show those products already own trace collection, datasets, evals, gateway controls, and hosted fine-tuning.
kolm_code_truth: Kolm has stronger differentiation as artifact/receipt/governance layer above them.
risk_or_opportunity: A generic dashboard or gateway roadmap would waste scarce engineering focus.
recommended_action: Build importers, route middleware, receipts, and artifact proof instead.
